Title: A GDSL lipase-like from Ipomoea batatas catalyzes efficient production of 3,5-diCQA when expressed in Pichia pastoris

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 454-based de novo RNA-seq library: PRJNA647243; proteomics (UHPLC-MS/MS) accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected from roots; studied in roots grown under nitrogen-free hydroponic conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Roots, especially under nitrogen deprivation context used for cloning
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen deprivation increased 3,5-DiCQA accumulation in roots; in vitro pH affected product profile (5-CQA optimal near pH 7, 3,5-DiCQA formation near pH 6)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; low catalytic efficiency considered insufficient to explain high in planta 3,5-DiCQA levels
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream/final step for 3,5-DiCQA formation from 5-CQA plus caffeoyl-CoA; also midstream in 5-CQA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms terminal product directly in vitro and also forms immediate precursor 5-CQA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; contains HXXXD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to quinate/shikimate; synthesizes 5-CQA from caffeoyl-CoA and quinic acid, and can acylate 5-CQA with caffeoyl-CoA to form 3,5-DiCQA; also broad activity toward cinnamoyl/p-coumaroyl acceptors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also produced 3,4-DiCQA likely by isomerization of 3,5-DiCQA; IbHQT showed only slight p-coumaroylquinic acid activity and no DiCQA production under tested conditions, so it is not a core gene
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited as sole engineering target for strong 3,5-DiCQA enhancement because activity toward DiCQA synthesis is weak; may be useful in combination with IbICS or for precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antihepatotoxic, neuroprotective, antiviral, ACE-inhibitory/hypotensive activities
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/chlorogenic acid and dicaffeoylquinic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chlorogenic acid/ dicaffeoylquinic acid branch of phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes 5-CQA formation and only low-efficiency 3,5-DiCQA synthesis in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; HPLC/UV/MS metabolite identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbHCT from Ipomoea batatas was cloned and identified as a BAHD acyltransferase ortholog. 2. Recombinant IbHCT efficiently synthesized 5-CQA from caffeoyl-CoA and quinic acid. 3. IbHCT also produced 3,5-DiCQA from 5-CQA plus caffeoyl-CoA in vitro, but only at low efficiency insufficient to explain high root accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: sweet potato; Nicotiana benthamiana; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ipomoea batatas; Nicotiana benthamiana; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT (transient plant expression, strong constitutive); pPICZαA AOX1 methanol-inducible in Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 454-based de novo RNA-seq library: PRJNA647243; proteomics by UHPLC-MS/MS accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from sweet potato tuber/root tissues with high 3,5-DiCQA synthase activity; enzyme activity enriched from tuber discs incubated 48 h in dark; transcript identified from roots under nitrogen deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Roots/tubers, especially nitrogen-deprived roots and induced tuber discs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen deprivation doubled 3,5-DiCQA in roots; tuber disc dark incubation induced activity; methanol induction enabled active secretion in Pichia pastoris; substrate concentration >10 mM 5-CQA inhibited bioconversion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as main actor/key final-step enzyme for 3,5-DiCQA synthesis in sweet potato, though additional enzymes may also contribute
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product from 5-CQA by chlorogenate-dependent caffeoyl transfer/disproportionation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL lipase-like protein; SGNH hydrolase; N-terminal signal peptide; conserved GDSL motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chlorogenic acid (5-CQA) into 3,5-DiCQA using 5-CQA as substrate; functions as isochlorogenate synthase/chlorogenate:chlorogenate caffeoyl transferase-like activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Active when produced in Nicotiana benthamiana and Pichia pastoris, but inactive in E. coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae under tested conditions, possibly due to solubility or post-translational modification; showed no activity with other tested acyl donors/acceptors; differences from earlier partially purified 25-kDa enzyme suggest additional enzymes may exist
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for metabolic engineering or industrial bioproduction of 3,5-DiCQA; suitable for heterologous production in Pichia; could be combined with upstream 5-CQA supply genes and optimized fermentation/immobilization strategies
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antihepatotoxic, neuroprotective, antiviral, ACE-inhibitory/hypotensive activities
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High substrate concentrations inhibit conversion; complete plant-level tradeoffs not reported
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal chlorogenic acid to dicaffeoylquinic acid conversion branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently promotes 3,5-DiCQA production from 5-CQA and enables high-yield bioconversion in Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secretory pathway/apoplast predicted; secreted into culture medium in Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteomic-transcriptomic identification; cloning; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; purified enzyme assay; UHPLC-MS product confirmation; kinetic characterization; heterologous bioconversion in Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbICS was identified by combining activity-guided protein purification, LC-MS/MS peptide sequencing, and root RNA-seq. 2. IbICS encodes a GDSL/SGNH hydrolase that efficiently converts 5-CQA into 3,5-DiCQA. 3. Functional enzyme activity was observed in Nicotiana benthamiana and Pichia pastoris, and Pichia achieved >60% conversion of supplied 5-CQA, supporting IbICS as the major final-step enzyme.
